---

# ipam_ip_range (Data Source)

Reads an existing IP range by name. Use this when one Terraform stack allocates a range and another stack only needs to read its CIDR — without creating a duplicate resource.

## Example Usage

```hcl
data "ipam_ip_range" "gke_nodes" {
  name = "prod-gke-nodes"
}

resource "google_container_cluster" "primary" {
  # ...
  cluster_ipv4_cidr = data.ipam_ip_range.gke_nodes.cidr
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Name of the IP range to look up.

### Read-Only

- `cidr` (String) The allocated CIDR block (e.g. `10.0.4.0/22`).
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels attached to the range.
